claim: Analysis pipeline can recover known mechanisms before neural use
key_evidence: Exp0 simulated recovery recovered frequency-only learnability-only three-factor and dependency-gated worlds
main_caveat: Only validates analysis pipeline not neural mechanism

claim: Sparse parity provides a quanta-style frequency baseline
key_evidence: B2 frequency effects strong and degree separates final performance
main_caveat: Acquisition coverage still lower than ideal

claim: B1 acquisition order is structurally predictable
key_evidence: H1 sign-stability across six configs; learnability robust; frequency weak but stable
main_caveat: One generated B1 family and small transformer scale

claim: Atomic parallel-rate models can select composite residual candidates
key_evidence: H2 selected simple atomic predictors and identified C06/C07 residuals
H3 shows C07 and A00→C06 do not support exact dependency
main_caveat: Residuals remain useful selectors but not proofs

key_evidence: A02→C06 exact pretraining and corruption beat same-operation and different-operation controls
main_caveat: Currently one positive pair/family; delay weaker

claim: Dependency is heterogeneous across formal components
key_evidence: A02→C06 positive; A00→C06 weak; A04→C07 operation-family; A03→C07 negative
A02→C06 exact gradient cosine 0.533 vs same-op 0.210 and different-op 0.223
main_caveat: CKA not discriminative and gradient is correlational

claim: Controlled B1 results causally explain Pythia/LLM training
key_evidence: No Pythia interventions or causal LLM training experiments
main_caveat: Can only do observational corroboration later
